top_findings: robots.txt returns HTTP 404 — crawl control absent
sitemap.xml returns HTTP 404 — no crawl discovery mechanism
Zero structured schema markup on any page — no app rich results possible
Inner page titles have brand duplication: 'X — Go Qatar | Go Qatar'
No canonical tags on any page — URL variation risk
quick_wins: Deploy sitemap.ts + robots.ts (already written, just git push)
Fix page title exports on contact, privacy-policy, terms pages (15 min)
Add alternates.canonical to layout.tsx and each page (20 min)
Add MobileApplication + Organization JSON-LD to layout.tsx (45 min)

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: HTTPS enforced with 2-year HSTS
Next.js pre-rendering via Vercel CDN (fast TTFB)
HTTP/2 enabled
lang=en on html element
Responsive viewport meta tag
Inter font WOFF2 preloaded
All images use next/image with explicit dimensions
Clean URL structure (/contact, /privacy-policy, /terms)

findings.title: robots.txt returns HTTP 404
findings.description: No robots.txt file at https://goqatar.app/robots.txt. Crawlers cannot confirm the sitemap URL. API routes have no disallow directive. Fix: deploy the already-written src/app/robots.ts.
findings.recommendation: git add src/app/robots.ts && git commit -m 'Add Next.js robots route' && git push

findings.title: sitemap.xml returns HTTP 404
findings.description: No XML sitemap at https://goqatar.app/sitemap.xml. Google cannot discover pages via sitemap submission. Fix: deploy the already-written src/app/sitemap.ts.
findings.recommendation: git add src/app/sitemap.ts && git commit -m 'Add Next.js sitemap route' && git push. Then submit in GSC.

findings.title: No canonical tags on any page
findings.description: Zero <link rel=canonical> tags. URL variations (trailing slash, query params) can split link equity. Add alternates.canonical to layout.tsx metadata and each page.
findings.recommendation: Add alternates: { canonical: '/' } to layout.tsx; add page-specific canonicals to /contact, /privacy-policy, /terms

findings.title: og:url hardcoded to homepage on all subpages
findings.description: Root layout openGraph.url is BASE_URL for all pages. Social shares of /contact, /privacy-policy, /terms show wrong URL and title.
findings.recommendation: Add per-page openGraph blocks to each inner page's metadata export

findings.title: 5 security headers missing
findings.description: Missing: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy. Only HSTS is present.
findings.recommendation: Add headers config to next.config.ts

findings.title: All homepage sections use 'use client'
findings.description: 9 sections including HeroSection, FeaturesSection, HowItWorksSection are client components for Framer Motion only. This increases JS bundle and reduces SSR content density.
findings.recommendation: Extract animation wrappers to separate client components. Keep static HTML in server components.

findings.title: Contact page query params create duplicate content risk
findings.description: /contact?tab=help and /contact?tab=feedback are linked in footer and may be indexed as separate pages.
findings.recommendation: Refactor to hash fragments (#help, #feedback) or add canonical pointing to /contact

findings.title: Web manifest purpose field incorrect
findings.description: Both PWA icons use 'purpose': 'any maskable' combined. W3C requires separate entries for 'any' and 'maskable'.
findings.recommendation: Split each icon into two entries with separate purpose values

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Meta description is strong and keyword-rich
HowItWorks section explains Qatar addressing system clearly
BusinessAPI section targets a distinct B2B audience
Privacy Policy and Terms pages exist and are dated
FAQ content on /contact is specific and genuinely helpful
App Store and Play Store links are correct and functional

findings.title: Inner page title duplication
findings.description: Layout template '%s | Go Qatar' applied to page titles already containing 'Go Qatar'. Rendered titles: 'Contact Us — Go Qatar | Go Qatar', 'Privacy Policy — Go Qatar | Go Qatar'.
findings.recommendation: Remove '— Go Qatar' from each inner page's title export. Use 'Contact Us', 'Privacy Policy', 'Terms & Conditions' — let the template append the brand once.

findings.title: H1 contains no target keywords
findings.description: Homepage H1 is 'Your City. Your Way.' — a brand tagline with zero search-intent keywords. Googlebot has no topical signal from the primary heading.
findings.recommendation: Add a keyword-bearing subtitle H2: 'Qatar's Address Finder — Navigate Zone, Street & Building'

findings.title: Fabricated social proof
findings.description: Hero shows generic colored circles and static stars with 'Loved by Qatar residents' — no review count, no rating number, no user names.
findings.recommendation: Display actual App Store rating using iTunes API (https://itunes.apple.com/lookup?id=6756709380)

findings.title: snapinfinity.com serves a Vite placeholder
findings.description: The rel=author link points to a blank scaffold page. LLMs and quality raters find nothing when validating author authority.
findings.recommendation: Build a minimal company profile page on snapinfinity.com with company description and link back to goqatar.app

findings.title: Gmail contact email undermines business credibility
findings.description: help.goqatar@gmail.com is visible on all legal pages and business inquiry form. Reduces trust for B2B inquiries.
findings.recommendation: Set up support@goqatar.app or hello@goqatar.app via Google Workspace or Resend

findings.title: Unsubstantiated superlatives in UpcomingSection
findings.description: 'Cheapest fares', 'Lowest fare guarantee', 'Cheapest cab guarantee' — unverifiable superlative claims Google quality raters flag.
findings.recommendation: Replace with 'competitive pricing' or remove claims until features launch with real data

findings.title: No blog or content marketing
findings.description: 4-page site with no editorial content. Zero topical authority signals. No ability to rank for awareness-stage queries.
findings.recommendation: Publish 'Qatar Zone Street Building Address System Explained' (1,500+ words) as foundational content

findings.title: Privacy Policy and Terms are thin
findings.description: ~400 words each. Missing: data retention periods, third-party SDK list, GDPR/PDPL applicability, cookie policy.
findings.recommendation: Expand with SDK disclosures (Google Maps, Google Sign-In), data retention table, and Qatar PDPL compliance statement

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Meta description is benefit-oriented and keyword-inclusive (157 chars)
All OG/Twitter Card metadata is complete
Theme color matches brand (#C9A84C gold)
Googlebot directive: index follow, max-image-preview:large
Category metadata: travel — correct

findings.title: No FAQ section on homepage
findings.description: No FAQ content targeting awareness queries. Good FAQ exists on /contact but not on homepage where most SEO traffic would land.
findings.recommendation: Add FAQ section with 6 Q&A pairs and FAQPage JSON-LD schema

findings.title: Only 1 of 8 H2s uses question form
findings.description: AI Overviews prefer question-based headings for extraction. 'Need the Go Qatar API for your business?' is the only one.
findings.recommendation: Reframe feature section H2s as questions: 'What can Go Qatar do?' or 'How does Go Qatar find your address?'

findings.title: No Twitter handle in meta tags
findings.description: twitter:creator and twitter:site tags are absent. If a Twitter/X account exists, add it for attribution.
findings.recommendation: Add twitter:site if a Go Qatar or Snap Infinity Twitter account exists

findings.title: Zero JSON-LD on any page
findings.description: No structured data found anywhere in the codebase. Google cannot classify this as an app, cannot generate rich results, and AI systems have no entity anchor.
findings.recommendation: Add MobileApplication + Organization + WebSite @graph to layout.tsx. Add BreadcrumbList to inner pages.

findings.title: No MobileApplication schema — missing rich results
findings.description: Without SoftwareApplication/MobileApplication schema, Google cannot display star ratings, price, or OS information in SERPs. All competitor app store listings inherently have this data.
findings.recommendation: Add MobileApplication JSON-LD block (see ACTION-PLAN.md Phase 1.5 for complete code)

categories.name: Performance (CWV)
what_works: Pages pre-rendered by Vercel (fast TTFB via CDN)
Inter font with WOFF2 preload and display:swap
Next.js Image component used throughout (auto-WebP, lazy loading)
SVG icons inlined (zero HTTP requests)
No render-blocking third-party scripts
Async JS chunk loading

findings.title: Hero H1 starts at opacity:0 — LCP risk
findings.description: HeroSection H1 uses initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 40 }} with 0.65s animation. LCP candidate is invisible until JS hydrates.
findings.recommendation: Ensure LCP candidate has a CSS fallback that renders it visible before JS. Consider non-animated initial state for SSR.

findings.title: Full Framer Motion bundle (~50-70KB gzipped)
findings.description: All components import from 'framer-motion' without tree-shaking optimization.
findings.recommendation: Use <LazyMotion features={domAnimation}> with <m.div> — reduces bundle to ~17KB

findings.title: Infinite animations run off-screen
findings.description: AppScreensSection clock hands and card animations use repeat: Infinity unconditionally — CPU waste when section is off-screen.
findings.recommendation: Wrap in whileInView instead of unconditional animate

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: SSR pre-rendering means AI crawlers can read content without JS
robots.ts (once deployed) allows all AI crawlers including GPTBot and ClaudeBot
HowItWorks section has the best citation-ready prose on the site
BusinessAPI section has a named taxonomy of use cases (quotable)
App Store and Play Store listings are independently indexed entities

findings.title: llms.txt missing
findings.description: No llms.txt at https://goqatar.app/llms.txt. AI crawlers cannot understand site structure or content citability from a machine-readable file.
findings.recommendation: Create /public/llms.txt with app description, feature list, and Qatar addressing explainer (template in ACTION-PLAN.md)

findings.title: No citable prose passages (134-167 words)
findings.description: All page copy is UI microcopy — short fragments optimized for visual scanning, not AI extraction. LLMs cannot cite this content reliably.
findings.recommendation: Add a 150-200 word prose paragraph explaining what Qatar's Zone/Street/Building system is and why Go Qatar solves it

findings.title: No FAQ with question-form headings
findings.description: Question-based headings directly map to LLM query patterns. Only 1 of 8 H2s uses question form. No FAQ section on homepage.
findings.recommendation: Add FAQ section with 6 questions targeting real LLM query patterns

findings.title: No YouTube presence
findings.description: YouTube presence has ~0.737 Spearman correlation with LLM citation frequency — highest of any signal tested.
findings.recommendation: Record 60-90 second app demo walkthrough, title it for Qatar address queries, embed on homepage

categories.name: Images
what_works: OG image exists and is accessible (https://goqatar.app/og-image.png)
OG image dimensions properly declared (1200x630)
OG image alt text: 'Go Qatar — Navigate Qatar effortlessly'
Twitter Card summary_large_image configured correctly
Multiple favicon sizes: 32px, 192px, 512px, 180px Apple touch
Next.js Image component used for auto-WebP conversion
SVG icons inlined (crisp, zero HTTP overhead)

findings.title: Hero badge app icon has empty alt text
findings.description: In HeroSection.tsx: <Image src='/logos/app_icon.svg' alt='' width={18} height={18} /> — the primary brand icon has no alt text.
findings.recommendation: Change alt='' to alt='Go Qatar app icon'

findings.title: OG image dimensions unverified
findings.description: og:image:width=1200 and og:image:height=630 are declared but the actual /og-image.png file dimensions have not been verified.
findings.recommendation: Verify with: curl -sI https://goqatar.app/og-image.png | grep content-length — then validate image dimensions with an image tool

findings.title: No real app screenshots for MobileApplication schema
findings.description: The MobileApplication schema screenshot property currently points to og-image.png. Real device screenshots (minimum 250px wide) would qualify for Google app rich results.
findings.recommendation: Add 2-3 real screenshots to /public/screenshots/ and reference them in schema

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Deploy sitemap.ts + robots.ts (already written — just git push)
Fix title duplication on /contact, /privacy-policy, /terms pages
Add canonical tags to layout.tsx and all 4 pages
Fix og:url on subpages (add per-page openGraph)
Add MobileApplication + Organization + WebSite JSON-LD to layout.tsx
Add security headers to next.config.ts
Add llms.txt to /public

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Replace fabricated social proof with real App Store rating
Add FAQ section to homepage (6 Q&A pairs + FAQPage JSON-LD)
Implement device-detection hero CTA (getDeviceStoreLink() already exists)
Add BreadcrumbList schema to inner pages
Fix site.webmanifest icon purpose field
Add Qatar address system explainer sentence to hero
Fix web manifest PWA icon purpose entries

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Publish 'Qatar Zone Street Building Address System Explained' guide (1,500+ words)
Rebuild snapinfinity.com with real company content
Create /business landing page for the API audience
Set up LinkedIn/Reddit presence for Snap Infinity
Record and publish YouTube demo video (60-90 seconds)
Set up professional domain email (support@goqatar.app)

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Submit sitemap in Google Search Console
Submit sitemap in Bing Webmaster Tools
Set up Google Analytics 4 for organic traffic tracking
Monitor GSC Coverage tab for indexation status
Monitor Core Web Vitals in GSC
Run /seo drift baseline after Phase 1 deploy
Check app store rating monthly to update schema aggregateRating

go_qatar_differentiators: Integrated Qatar news feed (3 view modes)
Both Google Maps and Waze navigation
Business API for enterprise address resolution
competitive_context.content_gap: No competitor has published an authoritative English-language guide explaining Qatar's Zone/Street/Building addressing system. This is a low-competition, high-opportunity content target.
